SeguroTools

Auto, home, and property

Auto, home, and property guides, tools, and source-backed answers.

How to use this category

Auto, homeowners, renters, flood, pet, jewelry, umbrella, and parametric coverage depend heavily on policy wording and state market conditions. The cheapest quote may leave a large exposure if limits, exclusions, deductibles, or disaster coverage are misunderstood.

  • Start with the declarations page and any endorsement schedule.
  • Check whether flood, earthquake, wildfire, jewelry, business use, or rideshare exposure is excluded.
  • Use state resources for complaint routing, rate notices, residual markets, and disaster guidance.

After reading a national guide, open the state source shelf to confirm current complaint, licensing, market, and disaster information.

State source shelf

  1. NAIC State Insurance Departments (NAIC)
  2. NAIC Consumer Resources (NAIC)
  3. HealthCare.gov (CMS)
  4. Medicare.gov (CMS)
  5. FEMA Flood Insurance (FEMA)

Use the state department for current complaint, licensing, rate, and market notices.

Umbrella policy education for excess liability, underlying auto and homeowners limits, exclusions, rental properties, teen drivers, lawsuits, and asset protection questions.

Wedding Insurance, Event Cancellation, and Vendor Risk

Wedding and special event insurance education for cancellation, postponement, liability, liquor exposure, vendor failure, deposits, weather, illness, and venue requirements.

Read guide

Jewelry Insurance, Appraisals, and Scheduled Property

Jewelry insurance education for appraisals, scheduled personal property, homeowners sublimits, mysterious disappearance, deductibles, replacement cost, and documentation.

Read guide

Parametric Insurance for Disaster Triggers

Parametric insurance education for trigger-based payouts, hurricanes, earthquakes, flood or weather events, basis risk, claim documentation differences, and disaster planning.

Read guide
